THE COURT DECLINES TO EXERCISE ITS DISCRETION TO DISMISS THIS PETITION FOR NOT HAVING BEEN BROUGHT TO TRIAL WITHIN TWO YEARS BECAUSE, AMONG OTHER REASONS, THE LAW PREFERS RESOLUTION ON THE MERITS, MANY OF THE SAME ISSUES WOULD STILL NEED TO BE ADDRESSED IN RESOLVING THE MOVING PARTY'S PETITIONS (WHICH SHE IS NOT OFFERING TO DISMISS), AND MOVING PARTY HERSELF SHARES SUBSTANTIAL RESPONSIBILITY FOR THE DELAY IN BRINGING THIS PETITION TO CONCLUSION. MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Timothy Barteau appeared by zoom for Maureen Magilavy.
Kristofer Biorn appeared by zoom for Kay Magilavy.

Mr. Barteau requested a 30-day continuance stating that they have had significant discussion, that he believes they have a resolution and that they are working on final language. Mr. Biorn agrees. The Petition for Order Appointing a Neutral Arbitrator, Petition to Appoint an Independent Successor Trustee, etc., Petition for Reimbursement of Trustee's Attorney's Fees, etc., Petition for Orders Determining Ownership of Trust Property, etc., Petition for Orders for Conveyance or Transfer of Property, etc. and Petition to Substitute Successor Trustee, etc. are continued to April 19, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Substitute Successor Trustee, Simone Thomas, In Place Of Deceased Respondent
Off Calendar Mar-15-2021 Continued To Apr-19-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Appointing Neutral Arbitrator; Exhibits
Off Calendar Feb-01-2021 Continued To Mar-15-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kristofer Biorn appeared by zoom for Kay Magilavy (Successor Trustee of the Exemption Trust under the Magilavy Family Trust).
Timothy Barteau appeared by zoom for Maureen Magilavy (individually and as the Administrator of the Estate of David Magilavy).

Mr. Barteau stated that they have taken efforts to preserve Maureen's rights as the Administrator of David's estate, that they filed a creditor's claim against the Estate of Simone Thomas and that there is the outstanding question of who the Trustee actually is Mr. Barteau also stated that they talked about settlement, that there is a counter offer, that there is a pending petition to appoint a third party and that he objects to Kay acting as the Trustee. In addition, Mr. Barteau stated that no one has substituted in as a successor in interest. Mr. Biorn stated that the case is not at issue yet, that at some point his Client will petition to substitute in in Simone's place, that they have been in settlement discussion and that he believes everything should be put over until the creditor's claim is addressed. The Court raised the issue of taking Simone's petitions off calendar. Mr. Biorn asked the Court to wait until his Client steps in. Counsel indicated that they want to resolve this amongst themselves. The Court informed Counsel that at some point it will require a joint status statement. The Petition for Order Appointing Neutral Arbitrator, the Petition to Appoint an Independent Successor Trustee, etc., the Petition for Reimbursement of Attorney's Fees. etc., the Petition for Orders Determining Ownership of Trust Property, the Petition for Orders for Conveyance or Transfer of Property Held by Trustee, etc. and the Petition to Substitute Successor Trustee are continued to March 15, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Aug-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Simone Thomas in Propria Persona.
Maureen Magilavy in Propria Persona.

Ms. Magilavy spoke of her health issues, stated that she has been trying to get a four month extension, that she sent two letters to the Court and that they were rejected. The Court explained its tentative ruling, advised Ms. Magilavy that the motion requires a response 9 court days before the hearing, that there are procedures that need to be followed when requesting continuances, that there are ways in which she can seek relief from this type of motion and urges her to get an Attorney to help her. Ms. Magilavy stated that she is fine with the ruling. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. The Motion for Order that Requests for Admission be Deemed Admitted and for Monetary Sanction is granted, no opposition having been filed and no substantially compliant responses to the requests for admission having been served prior to the hearing date. The requests, to the extent they are within the scope of CCP section 2033.010, are deemed admitted, and Ms. Magilavy is ordered to pay Ms. Thomas sixty dollars as sanctions. Ms. Thomas is to provide a proposed order that is consistent with the tentative ruling.

Petitioners Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Seven And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-15-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Simone Thomas in Propria Persona.
Maureen Magilavy appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

The Parties provided trust history and presented oral argument to support their positions. Upon conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling stands as follows: Petitioner's motion is denied. Responding party objected to the requests for production by stating that she had previously produced all documents related to the relevant trust and that she would not produce other documents because they contained private financial information. There is no reason to order production of documents to which petitioner already has access through other productions. The requests were each dramatically overbroad, and petitioner has failed to show that documents which have not been produced are either relevant or reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of relevant information. Exploring a statement that may have been made in the course of mediation cannot be a basis for finding relevance. Even if petitioner had demonstrated relevance, the requested documents include privileged personal financial interests and tax records as to which petitioner has not shown a sufficient countervailing interest to overcome the privilege. Ms. Magilavy is to prepare a form of order, present it to Ms. Thoms for approval as to form and provide it to the Court

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Off Calendar Sep-04-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jenni Harmon for and with Maureen Magilavy.
Simone Thomas in Propria Persona.
Kay Magilavy also present.

Ms. Harmon stated that she is still meaning to withdraw as Counsel. All parties with the exception of Jenni Harmon and Maureen Magilavy were asked to vacate the courtroom. An in camera hearing was held. Upon conclusion, the Court granted the Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel (subject to review and receipt of a revised order). Ms. Thomas indicated that they may be able to settle this matter with out the help of Counsel. The Court expressed concern about both Parties being self represented, spoke of the repetitive petitions that have been filed as well as the overlapping requests, urged the parties to put aside their emotional feelings and try to get to the bottom of this, urged them to get Attorneys and suggested mediation. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than October 26, 2018. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by October 31, 2018. The 1) Petition for interpretation of Terms of Trust, etc. 2) Petition for Orders Determining Ownership of Trust Property, etc., 3) Amended Petition for Orders for Conveyance, etc., 4) Petition for Reimbursement of Attorney's Fees, etc. 5) Amended Petition to Substitute Successor Trustee, etc., 6) Petition to Appoint an Independent Successor Trustee, etc., and 7) Petition for Order Appointing Neutral Arbitrator, etc. are continued to and the Parties are to return on November 13, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings For Simone Thomas S Petition For Interpretation Of Trust And Relief From Breach Of Trust Filed February 26, 2016; Supporting Memorandum
Off Calendar May-30-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Harmon appearing via Courtcall for Maureen Magilavy (respondent). Petitioner in Propria Persona Simone Thomas.

Simone presented oral argument in support of her motion for judgment. Ms. Harmon raised the question of imposing sanctions. The Court stated that it will stand by its' tentative ruling and not issue sanctions at this time. In addition the court denied Ms. Harmon's request to continue the hearings set on June 5, 2018.

Having heard oral argument, the Court adopts its' tentative ruling as follows: Denied. The issues presented in this motion were previously presented by Petitioner's motion for judgment on the pleadings heard and denied on September 6, 2017 (and many of those issues have also been presented (and ruled against) in other motions filed by Petitioner in these related petitions). The Court declines to consider this repetitive second motion for judgment on the pleadings, and the time to file a motion to reconsider has long since passed. Even were the Court to consider the motion on the merits, it would be denied for the same reasons Petitioner's earlier motions have been denied. Should Petitioner further delay these proceedings by filing any future non-meritorious motions raising already-addressed issues, the Court will seriously consider imposing sanctions.

Ms. Harmon to prepare a form of order that conforms to the tentative ruling.

Judge Peter J. Busch., Clerk: N. Wong, Bailiff: V. Jung. Not Reported.

Petition To Substitute Successor Trustee, Simone Thomas, In Place Of Deceased Respondent
Off Calendar May-01-2018 Continued To Jun-05-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Simone Thomas in propria persona
Jennifer Harmon for Maureen Magilavy via Court Call

The Court noted there are repetitive motions and petitions in this case that do not advance to results, and parties have not talked to each other. Ms. Harmon requested that Simone Thomas' Petition to Substitute Successor Trustee be denied since Maureen Magilavy has been appointed executor in Arizona. Ms. Thomas stated that the role as Successor Trustee are separate than Executor of Estate, and she thought marshalling assets of exempt trust would be resolved. The Court stated that parties should talk to agree on a neutral trustee, even on a temporary basis, who would have undisputed authority to marshal assets. Ms. Harmon stated that she attempted to meet and confer regarding appointment of an independent Trustee but Ms. Thomas was unwilling. Ms. Thomas stated that she is not prepared to accept the individual proposed by Maureen Magilavy but would like to research other professional independent Trustees. The Court directed Ms. Thomas to also reconsider her Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings as it has no advantage over her demurrer which the Court denied. The 1)Petition for interpretation of Terms of Trust, etc. 2)Petition for Orders Determining Ownership of Trust Property, etc., 3)Amended Petition for Orders for Conveyance, etc., 4)Petition for Reimbursement of Attorney's Fees, etc. 5)Amended Petition to Substitute Successor Trustee, etc., and 6)Petition to Appoint an Independent Successor Trustee, etc., are continued to June 05, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties are directed to meet and confer re: appointment of an independent, 3rd party Trustee.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Off Calendar Apr-18-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Harmon (appearing via CourtCall) for and Maureen Magilavy (respondent). Petitioner in Propria Persona Simone Thomass is present.

Simone presented oral argument in support of her position to her demurrer. Ms. Harmon reported that she does not contest to the tentative ruling.

Having heard oral argument and reviewing the court filed documents, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: The demurrer is overruled. A demurrer assumes the truth of the petition's allegations and cannot be premised on the respondent's assumption that she will prevail on her contentions. As pleaded, petitioner clearly has standing as either a beneficiary, a trustee, or both. Respondent will have the opportunity during the litigation to try to prove the invalidity of the later amendments on which this petition relies.

Ms. Harmon to prepare a form of order that conforms to the tentative ruling.

Ms. Thomas stated that she had received a will naming her as personal representative of the estate, upon which she filed a petition for probate in Pimal County Arizona. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Respondents 3-30-2016 Petition For Orders Determining Ownership Of Trust Property: Directing Third Party To Account For Trust Property: Imposing A Constructive Trust; And Directing Transfer Of Property To Trust; For Summary Adjudication Of The Affirmative Defenses Of Unclean Hands And Unjust Enrichment In Respondents 3-30-2016 Response To Petition For Interpretation Of Terms Of Trust; And For Summary Adjudicat
Off Calendar Sep-06-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

There being no appearances, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Denied. Petitioner's separate statement is not in the form required by CRC 3.1350(d)(1) in that it does not track the claims and defenses listed in the notice of motion and the seperate statement addreses discrete issues many of which would not resolve an entire claim or defense, and the motion is denied on that basis. In any event, Petitioner has not carried her initial burden of demonstrating that Respondents cannot prove its claims or defenses, and the motion is denied on that basis. In any event, Respondents have raised triable issues of material fact on several issues necessary to the motion, including at least the existence of an ownership agreement, the validity of signed agreements, and the existence of any fraud, and the motion is denied on this additional basis. To the extent the motion seeks adjudication of claims in Petitioner's November 22, 2016 Petition for Orders, which has since been amended, the motion is also denied as moot.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Matter Not Reported.

Demurrer To Beryl Magilavys Petition
Off Calendar Apr-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance by any attorney, any party, or any interested party.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: The demurrer of Respondent Maureen Magilavy is sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth and fifth causes of action, which assert no cognizable claim and seek no available remedy, but only seek to prevent Respondent from even asserting certain affirmative defenses, the merits of which the Court cannot adjudicate before they are asserted. The demurrer to the fifteenth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend because only the trustee can be compelled to address the internal affairs of the trust. The demurrer to the sixteenth cause of action is overruled because Respondent is arguably a necessary or appropriate party to some of the requested relief by virtue of her position in the chain of title as to some of the real property as to which injunctive relief is sought. In all other respects, the demurrer is sustained with 15 days leave to amend to allege facts that would support each element of aider and abettor liability as to each of the remaining claims.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Court Clerk: Nancy Wong; Not Reported.

David Magilavys Demurrer To Beryl Magilavys Petition
Off Calendar Apr-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance by any attorney, any party, or any interested party.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: The demurrer of Respondent David Magilavy is sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth and fifth causes of action, which assert no cognizable claim and seek no available remedy, but only seek to prevent Respondent from even asserting certain affirmative defenses, the merits of which the Court cannot adjudicate before they are asserted. The demurrer is overruled as to the remaining causes of action on the basis of Petitioner's representation that she is making claims against Respondent exclusively in his capacity as trustee (which is consistent with the language of the petition) and is making no claims against him in any other capacity. The Court construes the petition as being so limited, and will hold Petitioner to her representation in this respect.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Beryl Magilavy in Propria Persona.
Stephen Duvernay appeared by telephone for David Magilavy (Trustee).

Mr. Duvernay stated that the majority of the competing claims are of legal contention, that the new petition is largely duplicative and that the matter is ready to set for a two day trial. Ms. Magilavy stated that the matter can not be set for trial, that she could not serve a third party respondent, explained difficulty with service, raised the issue of limited discovery and stated that she is requesting an accounting. Ms. Magilavy also stated that she would like to subpoena the real estate company that sold the property and get other interrogatories out. The Court reminded Ms. Magilavy that at the last hearing she stated that she was not going to do any discovery. The Court also stated that it is not prepared to set a trial in light of a party not being served. The Petition for Orders for Conveyance or Transfer of Property Held by Trustee Claimed to Belong to Another, etc., Petition for Orders Determining Ownership of Trust Property, etc. and the Petition for Interpretation of Terms of Trust, etc. are continued to February 21, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

Petition For Orders Determining Ownership Of Trust Property; Directing Third Party To Account For Trust Property; Imposing A Constructive Trust; And Directing Transfer Of Property To Trust; Claims For Conversion And Restitution

Petition For Interpretation Of Terms Of Trust And Relief From Breach Of Trust (Probate Code 17200,
Off Calendar Dec-13-2016 Continued To Jan-03-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Orders Determining Ownership Of Trust Property; Directing Third Party To Account For Trust Property; Imposing A Constructive Trust; And Directing Transfer Of Property To Trust; Claims For Conversion And Restitution
Off Calendar Oct-11-2016 Continued To Dec-13-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Beryl Magilavy in Propria Persona. Stephen Duverny (via Court Call) for (trustee) David Magilavy.

The Court stated that the parties had agreed to mediate but that nothing has happened. Ms. Magilavy stated that her 2nd status report proposed that a special master be appointed. The Court asked if there was agreement to that. Mr. Duverny stated that they object to the cost of a special master and that it would not be efficient. The Court stated that it is not clear if it is any more efficient than an actual trial, asked if the parties could put aside their differences to meet and resolve the issues. Ms. Magilavy stated that she found 2 mediators, one of which was proposed by the trustee but that she never heard back from the trustee. Mr. Duverny stated that the main issue is the scope of the mediation and that they do not think it will be efficient or be able to resolve without the Arizona issues. Ms. Magilavy stated that the Arizona complaint parties are not involved in California. The Court stated that if the parties want to litigate and not mediate they need to go on that track. Ms. Magilavy stated that it would be helpful if the trustee can put aside proposals in Arizona and account for the management of the estate. The Court stated that there are many logistic issues, that they need to either litigate or meet & confer and that they need to compromise. Ms. Magilavy stated that the trustee should back down. Mr. Duverny stated that the trustee's position is that he is willing to mediate but must mediate all of the issues and that they understand from Ms. Magilavy that she wants to litigate the Arizona issues in Arizona. The Court stated that what would be left to do is to bring the California issues to trial and asked if the parties are ready to set trial. Mr. Duverny stated that they will need time for and plan to do financial discovery in all areas where Ms. Magilavy has used trust funds. Ms. Magilavy stated that she does not plan to do discovery at this time. The Court stated that it does not make sense to break this apart, that it will continue the matter for the parties to meet & confer and to submit a statement regarding the issues and how to resolve them no later than December 6, 2016, that it encourages the parties to figure out how to talk to each other and that they should be trying to figure out how to resolve the issues so that they don't spend time and money to figure this out. The Petition for Orders Determining Ownership of Trust Property, etc.& The Petition for Interpretation of Terms of Trust and Relief from Breach of Trust were continued to December 13, 2016 in Dept. 204 at 9:00 a.m.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Jun-07-2016 Continued To Aug-09-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Beryl Magilavy, in pro per; Stephen Duvernay for trustee David Magilavy.

The Court noted that there were numerous issues presented by the two petitions before him, and inquired if the case is amenable to resolution by mediation. Both Mr. Duvenay and Ms. Magilavy agree to try mediation. The Court notes that because of the value of the trust assets the matter would not qualify for the Court's pro bono mediation program, but said there are other good mediator services available. He also encouraged Ms. Magilavy to obtain the services of legal counsel to assist her. The two petitions before the Court were continued to August 9, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court also set a status hearing re mediation status for the same time and date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy John Boncato. (Not reported)
